title: Fermi Velocity
summary: The Fermi velocity is the slope of the dispersion at a crossing and acts as the effective light-cone speed in the linearized continuum description.
assumptions: The dispersion is differentiable at the crossing.
regime: Low-energy analysis near a Fermi point.
scope: Lecture One one-dimensional linearization and its relativistic analogy.

source_anchors.notes: Defined as the derivative of epsilon with respect to momentum at p0.

representation: Slope of the energy-momentum relation at the crossing.
failure_modes: Vanishing slope invalidates the simple chiral continuum description.
retrieval_hints: Use for the relativistic-form interpretation of a linearized band crossing.
